topic: family-free sharp identified set for probabilities of causation with continuous outcomes under a Kendall-tau band on potential-outcome dependence: characterize the sharp general-probability-of-necessity (GPN) set by DIRECT pointwise optimization of the copula value C(u1,u0) over all copulas whose Kendall's tau lies in a given band, using the closed-form Nelsen tau-constrained pointwise copula bounds; pair it with a doubly-robust efficient-influence-function estimator of the set endpoints and interval inference over the tau-indexed identified set. Differs from the one-parameter Gaussian-copula rho-interval of Chaoge-Han-Liu-Wu (arXiv:2605.01883) by being family-free and carrying the estimation/inference rung the anchor lacks

open_problems.open_problem: Characterize the family-free sharp GPN identified set obtained by optimizing C(u1,u0) over all copulas whose Kendall's tau lies in a fixed band.

source_refs.claim: Introduces continuous-outcome GPN, sharp Frechet/monotonicity bounds, and a copula sensitivity framework, but uses copula-model/association sensitivity rather than the full class of copulas satisfying a Kendall-tau band.
source_refs.author: Jile Chaoge, Kesen Han, Fahui Liu, Peng Wu

source_refs.claim: Reviews pointwise best-possible copula bounds under fixed concordance measures and contrasts Gini-gamma quasi-copula bounds with known Kendall-tau, Spearman-rho, and Blomqvist-beta bounds.
source_refs.author: Manuel Ubeda-Flores

source_refs.claim: Develops local copula-bound methods for concordance restrictions and points to existing local bounds for Kendall's tau, Spearman's rho, and Blomqvist's beta.
source_refs.author: Damjana Kokol Bukovsek, Tomaz Kosir, Blaz Mojskerc, Matjaz Omladic
source_refs.venue: Journal of Computational and Applied Mathematics

source_refs.excerpt: main open identification claim: the nontrivial point is no-gap constrained transport
sharp identified interval claim in Conjecture 1 is not confirmed

open_problems.why_unsolved: The literature supplies the two separate ingredients but not their econometric composition: Chaoge-Han-Liu-Wu define continuous GPN and use copula sensitivity, while the copula-bound literature gives pointwise extremal values for copulas with fixed concordance information. The in-repo Spearman-band predecessor was downgraded because weak-duality and outer envelopes did not prove endpoint attainment or no-gap sharpness. A Kendall-tau formulation is narrower because GPN depends on one copula value C(u1,u0), but sharpness still requires proving that the Nelsen-style pointwise extrema are attainable by a valid potential-outcome joint law satisfying the tau band.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A sharp partial-identification theorem expressing the lower and upper GPN endpoints as the lower and upper feasible values of C(u1,u0) over copulas with tau in [tau_L,tau_U], including an attainability proof for the causal potential-outcome law.

writing_exemplar.why: It is the closest motivation arc for continuous-outcome GPN and already frames copula dependence as the missing cross-world ingredient.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: binary probabilities of causation are attribution targets -> continuous outcomes need GPN and partial identification -> copula sensitivity tightens cross-world bounds -> replace parametric copula sensitivity by the family-free Kendall-tau sharp set

method_exemplars.technique: Pointwise best-possible copula-bound construction under a fixed concordance measure, with attention to whether the envelope is a copula or only a quasi-copula.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The desired GPN endpoint is exactly a point evaluation C(u1,u0) under a concordance restriction, so the same extremal-copula attainability logic is the bottleneck.

method_exemplars.technique: Local concordance-bound analysis for copula classes, including the proof discipline for best-possible pointwise bounds.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It supplies the copula-theory bridge between a scalar concordance restriction and pointwise bounds usable in a causal sharpness theorem.

method_exemplars.technique: Plug-in estimation and inference for sharp distribution-of-treatment-effects bounds built from marginal counterfactual distributions.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It is the nearest econometric template for turning potential-outcome marginal estimates plus dependence restrictions into bound endpoint inference.

open_problems.open_problem: Derive a doubly robust efficient-influence-function estimator and interval inference for the lower and upper endpoints of the Kendall-tau-band GPN set.

source_refs.claim: Derives efficient influence functions and semiparametric efficiency bounds for PN and PS under point-identifying binary-outcome assumptions.
source_refs.author: Zhaoqing Tian, Peng Wu

source_refs.claim: For ordinal outcomes and fixed parametric copulas, derives EIFs, one-step estimators, rate double robustness, semiparametric efficiency, and pointwise confidence bands.
source_refs.author: Peiyu He, Fan Li

source_refs.claim: Provides sharp partial-identification sensitivity bounds and estimators with double-sharpness, double-validity, and semiparametric-efficiency properties for ATE bounds.
source_refs.author: Jacob Dorn, Kevin Guo, Nathan Kallus

source_refs.excerpt: the m_n-out-of-n bootstrap estimates it consistently
state the candidate directional derivative or active-dual-face formula
future work here needs a genuinely new correction/calibration rule

open_problems.why_unsolved: Existing EIF work either treats binary PN/PS under point identification or ordinal potential-outcome comparisons under a fixed parametric copula. Sharp sensitivity work gives double-sharp estimators for different ATE bounds, not cross-world copula endpoint maps. The prior concordance-band proposal explicitly left the endpoint limit law open and reviewers asked for a concrete derivative or active-dual-face formula rather than a generic bootstrap claim. The tau-band GPN endpoint is a nonsmooth composition of estimated CDF thresholds, treatment nuisances, and piecewise Nelsen-bound formulas, so the open obstacle is the exact pathwise or directional derivative and the corresponding valid confidence construction.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: An estimation-and-inference theorem giving endpoint influence functions or directional derivatives, a cross-fitted doubly robust one-step estimator, variance or bootstrap calibration, and coverage for the scalar endpoint interval under regular and kink regimes.

writing_exemplar.why: It frames the move from unidentifiable potential-outcome dependence to copula-indexed estimands and then immediately derives EIF-based estimation.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: ordinal potential-outcome comparison depends on unidentified joint law -> fixed copula association turns the estimand into an observed-data functional -> EIF and one-step estimation make sensitivity curves inferential -> tau-band GPN needs endpoint EIFs for a family-free partial-ID curve

method_exemplars.technique: Efficient influence function derivation for copula-indexed potential-outcome functionals plus cross-fitted one-step estimation with rate double robustness.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The nuisance structure is the same observed-data problem, but the target changes from a fixed-copula estimand to a bound endpoint.

method_exemplars.technique: Double-sharp endpoint estimation for sharp sensitivity bounds, with validity under nuisance misspecification and efficiency under correct specification.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The desired result must prove that an estimated sharp bound remains sharp and inferentially valid, not merely that a plug-in endpoint is consistent.

method_exemplars.technique: Inference for suprema/infima of estimated functions using bias correction and Gaussian-process approximation for intersection bounds.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Endpoint maps over tau and over active copula-bound pieces have the same nonregular infimum/supremum structure.

open_problems.open_problem: Give sharp covariate-conditional and aggregated GPN tau-band bounds when the Kendall restriction is conditional on X, constant across X, or only unconditional.

source_refs.claim: Uses copula models for potential-outcome dependence and notes that covariate-dependent dependence parameters are difficult to specify in sensitivity analysis.
source_refs.author: Peiyu He, Fan Li

source_refs.claim: Defines continuous-outcome GPN through conditional potential-outcome laws before aggregating over observed covariates.
source_refs.author: Jile Chaoge, Kesen Han, Fahui Liu, Peng Wu

source_refs.claim: Supplies unconditional pointwise concordance-bound ingredients, leaving covariate-indexed causal aggregation outside its scope.
source_refs.author: Manuel Ubeda-Flores

source_refs.excerpt: continuous-X nuisance smoothing is left for a later version
No measurable argmax or argmin selector is asserted
not fully specified for continuous X

open_problems.why_unsolved: Nelsen-style pointwise copula bounds are unconditional mathematical statements, while continuous-outcome GPN is naturally formed conditionally on covariates and then averaged. A conditional tau(X) band, a constant conditional tau, and an unconditional tau band are not the same restriction, and the prior Spearman-band proposal explicitly avoided continuous-X smoothing and measurable selector claims. Even if the pointwise endpoint is closed form, the causal sharpness theorem must specify the restriction's level and prove that conditional endpoint functions are measurable and aggregate without losing sharpness.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A set of identification theorems comparing conditional tau(X)-band, constant conditional-tau, and unconditional-tau restrictions, with measurable conditional endpoint maps, aggregation formulas, and examples showing when the resulting GPN sets differ.

writing_exemplar.why: Its GPN formulation already passes through conditional laws and covariate aggregation, making it the natural exposition template.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: GPN is defined for observed covariate strata -> treatment assignment identifies marginal potential-outcome laws by stratum -> cross-world dependence remains unspecified -> the level at which Kendall tau is restricted determines the sharp aggregated set

method_exemplars.technique: Pointwise concordance-bound formulas applied stratumwise, with explicit tracking of whether each pointwise bound is attainable by a copula.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Conditional sharpness is obtained only if each stratum's copula-value extremum can be represented by an admissible conditional law.

method_exemplars.technique: Cross-fitted nuisance estimation with product-rate conditions for orthogonal causal functionals.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The continuous-X version needs nuisance-rate conditions for conditional CDF and propensity components before endpoint aggregation can be inferential.

open_problems.open_problem: Construct uniformly valid inference for the full tau-indexed GPN identified set when endpoint curves have active-bound switches, flat regions, or nonunique crossings.

source_refs.claim: Develops estimation and inference for intersection bounds defined by infima or suprema over potentially infinite function or constraint classes.
source_refs.author: Victor Chernozhukov, Sokbae Lee, Adam M. Rosen

source_refs.claim: Provides pointwise confidence bands along fixed-copula sensitivity curves, not uniform coverage for a family-free concordance-band identified set with active-form switches.
source_refs.author: Peiyu He, Fan Li

source_refs.claim: Revisits confidence intervals for scalar partially identified parameters and emphasizes procedures that remain valid when sample analog identified sets are awkward or empty.
source_refs.author: Jorg Stoye

source_refs.excerpt: does not claim ... a scalar monotone rho^star without additional single-crossing conditions
The straddling case is only an outer-envelope statement
showing when the envelope is sharp for one full-data law

open_problems.why_unsolved: Pointwise copula sensitivity curves and scalar interval-identified inference do not automatically yield coverage for the whole envelope indexed by tau. The prior concordance-band run explicitly refused a scalar monotone threshold without single-crossing assumptions, and related rejected copula-tube work warned that separately optimized envelopes may not paste into one sharp law. For Kendall-tau GPN, the closed-form bound can switch formula across tau values and may produce flat or nonunique threshold sets, so the open issue is a uniform set-valued inference object rather than a pointwise Gaussian-copula rho curve.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A uniform confidence theorem for the tau-indexed endpoint curves and their envelope, including a set-valued crossing or robustness-threshold definition and valid calibration under active-bound switches and nonunique crossing regimes.

writing_exemplar.why: It cleanly motivates why inference for an infimum or supremum over an index set needs different treatment from pointwise plug-in inference.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: many partial-ID parameters are extrema over indexed functions -> naive plug-in extrema are biased and nonregular -> valid inference needs uniform Gaussian/bias-correction machinery -> tau-indexed GPN endpoints need the same treatment with copula-bound switches

method_exemplars.technique: Strong-approximation and bias-correction methods for infimum/supremum bounds over rich index sets.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The tau-band endpoint is an extremum over an estimated sensitivity curve, so intersection-bound calibration is structurally analogous.

method_exemplars.technique: Confidence interval construction for partially identified scalar parameters that avoids pathological empty or too-short intervals.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The final GPN object is an interval and can have near-empty or kinked sample analogs when endpoint estimates cross.

method_exemplars.technique: Casewise pointwise copula-bound characterization under concordance constraints.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Uniform inference must track which identification formula is active as tau varies.

literature_map:
Tian-Pearl established classical binary probabilities-of-causation bounds and identification; Tian-Wu later derived efficient PN/PS inference in point-identified binary regimes.
Kawakami-Kuroki-Tian extend PoC definitions to continuous and vector variables under structural identifying assumptions.
Chaoge-Han-Liu-Wu introduce continuous-outcome GPN, derive sharp Frechet/monotonicity bounds, and add a copula-based sensitivity framework.
The unresolved identification obstacle is the cross-world joint law of (Y(1),Y(0)); observed data identify marginal potential-outcome laws but not their copula.
He-Li use fixed parametric copulas for ordinal causal estimands and derive EIFs, rate double robustness, efficiency, and pointwise sensitivity confidence bands.
The copula-theory literature contains pointwise best-possible bounds for copulas with fixed concordance information, including Kendall's tau; Ubeda-Flores 2025 and Kokol Bukovsek et al. 2021 are useful modern entry points.
What is missing is the econometric bridge from those pointwise Kendall-tau copula bounds to the continuous-GPN ratio/endpoint functional.
Dorn-Guo-Kallus provide a sharp-sensitivity and double-validity template, but for ATE sensitivity to unmeasured confounding rather than cross-world dependence between potential outcomes.
Chernozhukov-Lee-Rosen provide the closest general inference machinery for intersection bounds and extrema over indexed functions.
Stoye and Imbens-Manski-style interval-inference work is relevant once the endpoint pair is identified, but it does not by itself solve tau-uniform active-bound switching.
The main live gaps are therefore family-free Kendall-tau sharpness, covariate-level interpretation of the tau restriction, DR/EIF endpoint estimation, and uniform inference over the tau-indexed identified set.
prior_proposal_map:
1. The directly relevant in-repo predecessor is pid_benefit_concordance_band_v1: continuous outcomes, potential-outcome copula dependence, rank-concordance bands, probability-of-benefit endpoints, and endpoint inference.
2. That proposal was downgraded because the sharp Spearman-band interval was not proved: reviewers found only weak-duality/outer-envelope results, no endpoint attainment, and missing measurable selectors.
3. Its sec11 Honest scope flags identify the main reusable gaps: no-gap constrained transport, finite-X-only estimation, active endpoint faces, no continuous-X nuisance smoothing, and no scalar monotone rho-star.
4. Reviewer JSON strengthening_paths repeatedly ask for concrete directional derivatives or active-dual-face formulas rather than generic bootstrap consistency.
5. pid_bound_intersection_inference_v1 is a negative inference precedent: another bootstrap/active-set story is not enough without a new calibration rule.
6. pid_ot_dte_marginal_v1 and pid_dose_response_iv_v1 warn that local copula/rank OT envelopes may fail to paste into one full-data law.
7. The current Kendall-tau GPN topic can cross-link to these prior gaps because it targets a narrower pointwise C(u1,u0) functional while still needing causal sharpness and inference.
8. Study-pipeline gaps appended to this invocation were IV/Manski/interval-regression oriented and were not emitted because they did not anchor the probability-of-causation tau-band topic.
